| Iac resource property extraction | 🟢 `partial` | — | 4194 | `internal/engine/iac_code_properties.go`<br>`internal/engine/pulumi_edges.go`<br>`internal/engine/pulumi_properties_test.go` | epic #4194: Pulumi now stamps a CURATED, bounded allow-list of literal SCALAR config props onto each resource's SCOPE.InfraResource, alongside (not replacing) the existing output_ref/depends_on edge mining. Pass 5 (pulumiTSResourceArgsRe TS / pulumiPyResourceArgsRe Python) feeds the args body to the shared stampScalarProps closure (pulumi_edges.go), which calls iacCodeExtractScalarProperties (iac_code_properties.go) and merges curated key=value onto the entity by logical name, right before applyPulumiArgEdges runs the edge mining (so both happen). Curated keys (case/underscore-folded, both camelCase + snake_case): instanceType/machineType/size/sku/tier/instanceClass/nodeType/vmSize, memorySize/memory/timeout, runtime/engine/engineVersion/version, count/desiredCapacity/desiredCount/minSize/maxSize/minCapacity/maxCapacity/replicas, port/protocol, allocatedStorage/storageType. ONLY literal scalars are stamped (quoted strings, numbers, true/false); Output/member-access refs (ami.id, sizing.nodeType) and object/array values are SKIPPED (they stay reference edges). Value-asserting tests pin exact key=value: TestPulumi_ScalarProps_TS (instanceType=t3.micro, count=3; ami=ami.id NOT stamped), TestPulumi_ScalarProps_TS_OutputValuedCuratedKeyNotStamped (port=6379 stamped; nodeType=sizing.nodeType curated-but-Output NOT stamped), TestPulumi_ScalarProps_Python (instance_type=t3.large, desired_capacity=2; ami output NOT stamped), TestPulumi_ScalarProps_NoRegressionOutputRefEdge (runtime=nodejs20.x + memorySize=1024 stamped AND output_ref fn→data edge still fires). PARTIAL: single-file regex over Pulumi-TS+Pulumi-Python; Pulumi-Go/C#/Java property stamping deferred. DEPLOY-DEFERRED. |
